text.title: activation-framework Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for activation-framework as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Optimizing for high-completion events rather than retention-correlated behaviors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agents pick "completed onboarding" as activation because it has 80% completion vs "created a report" at 25%. But only the latter predicts retention. Always force the agent to score activation event candidates against D30 retention lift.

failure-mode.description: Using "time-to-activation" without a fixed window definition.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Leaving the window open inflates activation rate and breaks week-over-week comparisons. Define window explicitly: e.g. "activated within 7 days of signup".

failure-mode.description: Attacking the biggest funnel drop-off without confirming it is causal.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Funnel drop-offs are correlated with activation failure but not always the direct cause. Confirm with session recordings and user interviews before building the fix.

failure-mode.description: Reducing signup friction before fixing downstream activation steps.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: More signups with the same downstream funnel just inflates the top-of-funnel number. Fix activation rate before expanding acquisition.
